channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Warriors to retire Andre Iguodala's No. 9 jersey
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — As Andre Iguodala moves around town in the Bay Area these days, he is regularly stopped by fans eager to praise him for how he carried himself while playing for the Golden State Warriors. For his grit and his drive. For doing all the little things many never saw.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alyssa Thomas heading to Mercury from Sun in trade, AP source says
item.description: The Phoenix Mercury were finalizing a deal to acquire Alyssa Thomas from the Connecticut Sun, a person familiar with the trade told The Associated Press on Tuesday night. The Sun also will send Ty Harris to Phoenix, while Connecticut will receive Natasha Cloud, Rebecca Allen and the No.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Scottie Scheffler details his hand injury from making ravioli and is ready to get back to golf
item.description: Scottie Scheffler figures the injury to his hand that kept him out of golf for a month could have been worse.
item.dc:creator: Doug Ferguson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Brittney Griner plans to sign with the Atlanta Dream, ending her 11-year run with Phoenix
item.description: Brittney Griner plans to sign with the Atlanta Dream, marking the end of her 11-year career with the Phoenix Mercury.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lions agree to hire Broncos' John Morton as OC and promote Kelvin Sheppard to DC, AP source says
item.description: The Detroit Lions have agreed to hire Denver Broncos assistant John Morton as offensive coordinator and promote Kelvin Sheppard to defensive coordinator.
item.dc:creator: Larry Lage, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ex-Bears coach Eberflus returns to Cowboys to run the defense; Sorensen takes over special teams
item.description: FRISCO, Texas (AP) — The Cowboys hired former Chicago Bears coach Matt Eberflus as defensive coordinator Tuesday for his second stint on the Dallas staff.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Legendary NASCAR broadcaster Bill Weber dead at 67
item.description: Bill Weber, the legendary NASCAR broadcaster who was a fan favorite during his time in the pit and the booth, died Dec. 13 at age 67. He retired in 2009.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bears hire Dennis Allen as defensive coordinator under new coach Ben Johnson
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. (AP) — The Chicago Bears have hired Dennis Allen as defensive coordinator and Declan Doyle as offensive coordinator under new coach Ben Johnson. Chicago also is keeping Richard Hightower as special teams coordinator as it looks to rebound after going 5-12 this season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lamar Jackson, Josh Allen and Jayden Daniels to skip Pro Bowl Games
item.description: Baltimore's Lamar Jackson, Buffalo's Josh Allen and Washington's Jayden Daniels won't be participating in the Pro Bowl Games this week. The NFL announced final rosters Tuesday that didn't include any of those quarterbacks.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Super Bowl LIX jersey colors announced for Chiefs-Eagles rematch
item.description: The jersey colors the Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles will wear for the Super Bowl have been revealed. The Eagles chose to wear "midnight green" again.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Falcons’ Jeff Ulbrich says lack of truth-telling, delegation led to failed stint as Jets interim head coach
item.description: Former New York Jets defensive coordinator Jeff Ulbrich said Monday that his failure to delegate and a seemingly lack of honesty led to his struggles as the Jets interim head coach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big-time Love: Arizona's Caleb Love hits half-court heave at buzzer in OT win over No. 3 Iowa State
item.description: TUCSON, Ariz. (AP) — Caleb Love trotted back, his head down, shoulders slumped after the latest of his missed 3-pointers clanged the back rim. This was not his night — until it was.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Surging Rockets beat the Celtics after knocking off the Cavaliers twice
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The Houston Rockets have been one of the NBA’s more surprising teams this season. After their past three games, they shouldn’t shock anyone.
item.dc:creator: KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Inside how Super Bowl footballs are made
item.description: As soon as this year's Super Bowl matchup was set, workers at the Wilson Sporting Goods football factory jumped into action.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Super Bowl 2025: Time, channel, halftime show, how to watch Chiefs vs. Eagles livestream
item.description: Patrick Mahomes, Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift will be among the familiar faces at the Super Bowl when the Kansas City Chiefs go for an unprecedented three-peat against the Philadelphia Eagles on Feb. 9 in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida coach Todd Golden cleared in Title IX investigation regarding stalking allegations
item.description: The University of Florida has cleared men's basketball coach Todd Golden following a four-month Title IX investigation into allegations of sexual exploitation, sexual harassment and stalking.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mahomes and the Chiefs will face Hurts, Barkley and the Eagles as they seek a Super Bowl three-peat
item.description: Patrick Mahomes, Travis Kelce and the Kansas City Chiefs are going for a three-peat in a Super Bowl rematch. This time, Jalen Hurts and the Philadelphia Eagles have Saquon Barkley. The Eagles and Chiefs will face off for the Lombardi Trophy for the second time in three years on Feb.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mahomes and the Chiefs will face Hurts, Barkley and the Eagles as they seek a Super Bowl three-peat
item.description: Patrick Mahomes, Travis Kelce and the Kansas City Chiefs are going for a three-peat in a Super Bowl rematch.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Chiefs get more Mahomes magic and advance to 3rd straight Super Bowl, beating the Bills 32-29
item.description: Patrick Mahomes threw for 245 yards and a touchdown, ran through the Buffalo defense for two more scores, and relied on his defense at key moments to help the Kansas City Chiefs beat the Bills 32-29 on Sunday night and reach the Super Bowl for the third consecutive season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jayden Daniels auctioning off custom jacket; proceeds to benefit LAFD
item.description: Jayden Daniels may be focusing on Sunday’s NFC Championship matchup against the Philadelphia Eagles, but he doesn’t forget about where he came from. In the wake of the devastating Los Angeles area wildfires, Daniels, a San Bernardino native, is auctioning off a personalized jacket.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Runnin' Utes get swept by Baylor, 76-61
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was a tough week offensively for the Utah basketball team. After scoring just 35 points against Houston, the Utes put up 61 in a 76-61 loss to Baylor Saturday at the Huntsman Center. Norchad Omier had 22 points and 12 rebounds, and V.J.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Kneepkens scores 32 as Utes hold off BYU, 81-76
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The last time Gianna Kneepkens played against BYU, she suffered a season-ending knee injury in December of 2023. This time around, Kneepkens had one of the best days of her career.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Student stampede injures several ahead of AU vs. Tennessee basketball game
item.description: AUBURN, Ala. (WRBL) – A chaotic scene unfolded Saturday morning outside Neville Arena as Auburn University students rushed to secure spots for the highly anticipated basketball game between #1 Auburn and #6 Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth White, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago faces Philadelphia on 4-game home slide
item.description: Philadelphia 76ers (16-27, 11th in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (19-26, 10th in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EST BETMGM SPORTSBOOK LINE: Bulls -3.5; over/under is 230.5 BOTTOM LINE: Chicago hosts Philadelphia looking to stop its four-game home slide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: LeBron James picked for 21st straight All-Star Game, extending NBA record
item.description: LeBron James is extending his All-Star records. And Giannis Antetokounmpo is the people’s choice, again. The NBA revealed the starters — some of them, anyway — for the revamped All-Star Game on Thursday night, and there wasn't much in the way of surprises.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Profar agrees to $42M, 3-year contract with Braves
item.description: Jurickson Profar agreed to a $42 million, three-year contract with the Atlanta Braves on Thursday, less than a year after the outfielder got a $1 million deal from the San Diego Padres with spring training already underway.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lawsuit says ex-Yankee Mariano Rivera failed to protect a girl from sexual abuse at a church camp
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Yankees legend Mariano Rivera and his wife are accused in a lawsuit of failing to protect a young girl who was sexually abused by an older child during a summer camp trip sponsored by their church.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ichiro Suzuki, CC Sabathia and Billy Wagner elected to Baseball Hall of Fame
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Used to leading off, Ichiro Suzuki got antsy when he had to wait. Considered a no-doubt pick for baseball's Hall of Fame and possibly the second unanimous selection, he waited by the phone for the expected call Tuesday. Fifteen minutes passed without a ring.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, WGN 9
